findings.description: The IMPL_FIX hardens two dangerous cases, but restore safety is still bypassable through a misconfigured profile. `_resolve_target_db()` rejects `railway_database_url` fallback and an explicit `--target-db` equal to `RAILWAY_DATABASE_URL`, yet it still returns `settings.database_url` unchecked at `src/cli/restore_commands.py:93-106`. If the active profile's `DATABASE_URL` is accidentally set to the Railway production URL, `aca manage restore-from-cloud` will still run `pg_restore --clean --if-exists` against prod without requiring `--allow-remote-target`. The added tests only cover the missing-`DATABASE_URL` fallback and explicit `--target-db` equality cases (`tests/cli/test_restore_from_cloud.py:242-305`); they do not cover `DATABASE_URL == RAILWAY_DATABASE_URL` or any other remote/non-local `DATABASE_URL`.
findings.resolution: Apply the same remote-target guard to the default `DATABASE_URL` path before returning it. At minimum, reject normalized equality between `database_url` and `railway_database_url`; preferably, reject any non-local/non-scratch target unless `--allow-remote-target` is set. Add tests for `DATABASE_URL == RAILWAY_DATABASE_URL` and for a remote-host `DATABASE_URL` without the override flag.

findings.description: The H3 parity fix switched both code paths to `overflow.ingested_at`, but the normative spec still requires `next_cursor` to be the last processed item's timestamp. `openspec/changes/cloud-db-source-of-truth/specs/content-references/spec.md:13-14,35` says callers paginate by advancing `since` to the last-processed content's `ingested_at`, while the HTTP route now returns the first unprocessed row at `src/api/routes/reference_routes.py:117-125` and the MCP in-process path mirrors that at `src/mcp_server.py:1910-1941`. The route test only asserts that `next_cursor` is non-null (`tests/api/test_reference_routes.py:335-361`), so validation does not catch the spec drift.
findings.resolution: Reconcile the spec with the implementation before merge. Either update `specs/content-references/spec.md` (and any related design text) to state that `next_cursor` is the first unprocessed row's `ingested_at`, or change both implementations and tests back to the last-processed-item rule.
